en_title: Telltale CEO Explains Why The Wolf Among Us 2 Has Taken So Long, And What Happened With That Split With Dispatch Maker Ad Hoc
cn_title: 《我们身边的狼2》为何难产？Telltale CEO详解
subtitle: 七年之痒，终见曙光
opus_summary: Telltale CEO详解《我们身边的狼2》开发长达近十年的原因，包括2018年工作室关闭、技术重置及与AdHoc Studio合作终止。游戏将于2027年发售，初代重制版今年晚些推出。

paragraphs.en: Nearly a decade on from Telltale Games' first announcement of a Wolf Among Us sequel, there's finally some good news. The rebooted version of Telltale has committed to a 2027 launch for Bigby Wolf's long-awaited next chapter, as well as a remastered version of his first adventure due out later this year .
paragraphs.cn: 距离 Telltale Games 首次宣布《我们身边的狼》续作已近十年，终于有了好消息。重获新生的 Telltale 承诺，大灰狼比格比·沃夫期待已久的下一章将于2027年发售，而他的首次冒险的重制版也将在今年晚些时候推出。

paragraphs.en: But what has taken so long? Well, the project has survived the shuttering of Telltale in 2018 and the brand's subsequent revival , a subsequent reboot , numerous delays , layoffs , and the now-dissolved partnership with Dispatch maker AdHoc Studio — which worked on the game for several years. But, as Telltale Games CEO Jamie Ottilie discusses with IGN below, that's only part of the story.
paragraphs.cn: 但为何耗时如此之久？该项目经历了2018年 Telltale 关闭、品牌随后复兴、再次重启、多次延期、裁员，以及与《超英派遣中心》开发商 AdHoc Studio 的合作终止——后者曾为这款游戏工作数年。然而，正如 Telltale Games 首席执行官 Jamie Ottilie 在下方与 IGN 的对话中所言，这只是故事的一部分。

paragraphs.en: Read on for detail on why Telltale restarted work on The Wolf Among Us 2 back in 2023, what became of the company's partnership with AdHoc, and how much of the Dispatch developer's work remains in the game — plus, Telltale's plans for the future, and whether we'll ever see a Wolf Among Us 3.
paragraphs.cn: 继续阅读，了解 Telltale 为何在2023年重启《我们身边的狼2》的开发、公司与 AdHoc 的合作关系如何演变、该开发商的工作有多少保留在游戏中——此外，还有 Telltale 的未来计划，以及我们是否能看到《我们身边的狼3》。

paragraphs.en: IGN: I'm looking forward to replaying The Wolf Among Us. Obviously there have been reports of a remaster, but it's definitely been long enough since the original.
paragraphs.cn: IGN：我很期待重玩《我们身边的狼》。显然已有重制版的报道，但距离初代发售确实已经很久了。

paragraphs.en: Jamie Ottilie: That's good to hear. Some people are on the fence about remasters and why would you be? It's 13 years old and we want to preserve it and make sure it runs on modern consoles and that new people can experience it. Why wouldn't we do it?
paragraphs.cn: Jamie Ottilie：很高兴听到你这么说。有些人对重制版持观望态度，但何必呢？这款游戏已经13年了，我们想保存它，确保它能在现代主机上运行，让新玩家也能体验。我们为什么不做呢？

paragraphs.en: And I guess you want the sequel to launch to as big an audience as possible and the first one coming back again helps grow that audience, 13 years on.
paragraphs.cn: 我想你们希望续作能面向尽可能多的受众，而13年后初代作品的回归有助于扩大这个受众群体。

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: Yeah, I can't believe it's been so long. The anniversary is this October. The remaster should be coming contemporous with the anniversary. Should be.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：是啊，真不敢相信已经过去这么久了。周年纪念在今年10月。重制版应该会与周年纪念同步推出。应该会。

paragraphs.en: Because it's been so long, fans have wondered if Wolf 2 will tell a completely different story to avoid alienating anyone — even if many hope at least some aspects continue from the first game to the second. What can you say there?
paragraphs.cn: 由于间隔太久，粉丝们想知道《狼2》是否会讲述一个完全不同的故事以避免疏远任何人——尽管许多人希望至少某些方面能从初代延续到续作。对此你有什么可以透露的吗？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: It's a new story but it's certainly a Bigby story. It's contemporous from a timeline standpoint with the first one. It's a true sequel. You don't need to play the first one to understand it, but it will help. It's the same universe, the same characters, fans should be very happy with what we're doing with the sequel.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：这是一个新故事，但无疑是大灰狼比格比的故事。从时间线来看，它与初代同时期。这是一部真正的续作。你不需要玩过初代就能理解，但玩过会有帮助。同样的宇宙，同样的角色，粉丝们应该会对我们在续作中所做的一切感到非常满意。

paragraphs.en: The original ended on something of a cliffhanger, or the suggestion of a reveal. Can you say whether that will be addressed in the sequel?
paragraphs.cn: 初代结局留下了悬念，或者说暗示了一个揭秘。你能说说续作是否会处理这个悬念吗？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: I can't say that right now. The other folks on my comms team are on this call will get very upset with me if I answer that question, but some of that will get covered at Summer Games Fest. I can say we do think fans will be very happy with what we're doing with the story and how we're respectfully treating some of the content from the first one.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：我现在不能说。如果我回答这个问题，通讯团队的其他同事会非常不高兴，但部分内容将在夏日游戏节上公布。我只能说，我们确实认为粉丝们会对我们在故事上的处理以及我们如何尊重地对待初代的一些内容感到非常满意。

paragraphs.en: Out of all the original Telltale games, why is the new Telltale so committed to The Wolf Among Us like this?
paragraphs.cn: 在所有原 Telltale 游戏中，为什么新的 Telltale 如此执着于《我们身边的狼》？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: Some of it's selfish [laughs]. I think it's important, it's one of my favorite games. One of the things that Mike and I bonded over is it's one of his favorite games. Quietly in conversations, you'll find that a lot of people will express that and put it in their top five or their top 10. It deserves a sequel. One of the reasons I chose to get involved with this iteration of Telltale was to see this sequel through. That level of desire is there and it isn't just me, it's everybody on my team. When I talked about putting Telltale back together with the folks who helped me do it, this was central to the conversation. So it's been there from the very beginning in terms of like, why are you doing this? What are you doing? Well, we're going to make this game, then we're going to figure the rest out.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：有一部分是出于私心（笑）。我认为这很重要，它是我最喜欢的游戏之一。我和 Mike 之所以能走到一起，部分原因就是这也是他最喜欢的游戏之一。私下交谈时，你会发现很多人都会这么说，把它列入前五或前十。它值得一部续作。我选择参与这一代 Telltale 的原因之一，就是要把这部续作做出来。这种渴望是存在的，而且不只是我，我团队里的每个人都是如此。当我和帮助我重建 Telltale 的人讨论时，这就是谈话的核心。所以从一开始，这个问题就一直存在：你为什么这么做？你在做什么？嗯，我们要做这款游戏，然后再考虑其他事情。

paragraphs.en: It's been a longer journey than any of us had hoped. It's hard for me to believe it's been seven years. It's been hard to be quiet the last few years while we got our stuff together and got the game going, but we're here now, it feels pretty good.
paragraphs.cn: 这段旅程比我们任何人希望的都要漫长。我很难相信已经过去了七年。过去几年里，当我们整理好一切、让游戏步入正轨时，保持沉默并不容易，但我们现在做到了，感觉很不错。

paragraphs.en: The Telltale brand still holds a lot of love from fans and there's a lot of curiosity around where the company as a whole is at. Can you get into the team's current size, and how much you're handling development internally? Obviously you've worked with Deck Nine on The Expanse, and then I believe it's Trick Studios you're now working with on Wolf 2?
paragraphs.cn: Telltale 品牌仍然深受粉丝喜爱，人们对公司整体状况充满好奇。你能谈谈团队目前的规模，以及内部承担了多少开发工作吗？显然你们曾与 Deck Nine 合作开发《苍穹浩瀚》，而我相信现在与你们合作《狼2》的是 Trick Studios？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: Yeah, Trick is our co-dev partner. We're working with Trick on a myriad of things and have been for a couple years now. One of the things that we decided to do as a company in terms of how we built this iteration of Telltale was to understand that development is cyclical. Your staffing meets are not consistent across a three-year period or a four-year period and rather than the boom bust cycle that we all went through in terms of hiring and laying off, that we would build intelligently around co-dev. So people that are internal to Telltale are really focused on leadership positions we know we're going to need for the long-term, structured around what the components of a Telltale game are and how we work effectively with co-dev partners to deliver that when you need to be at scale making content.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：是的，Trick 是我们的联合开发伙伴。我们与 Trick 合作了诸多事务，已经有好几年了。作为公司，我们在构建这一代 Telltale 时决定要做的一件事，就是认识到开发具有周期性。人员配置在三年或四年的周期内并不一致，与其经历我们都曾经历过的招聘和裁员的繁荣-萧条周期，我们选择围绕联合开发进行明智的构建。因此，Telltale 的内部人员真正专注于我们知道自己长期需要的领导职位，这些职位围绕 Telltale 游戏的组成部分以及我们如何与联合开发伙伴有效合作来构建，以便在需要大规模制作内容时能够交付。

paragraphs.en: That's been part of our business plan really from the outset. We did The Expanse externally with Deck Nine because we acknowledged that trying to do two games at the same time with a company from scratch is really not a great business plan. There's always been the understanding that you have to focus on what you can do. It's taken us longer than we intended because we did a tech reset in 2022. We originally started off with the legacy Telltale tool [and] Unreal Engine 4, and on paper that looked like [it would provide] all sorts of good production gains and tools and scalability and iteration. And a couple years into that journey the reality that like, 'no, that's not going to be true' became obvious. And so we did a reset on pipeline and went back to square one in terms of how we were building the content, not what content we were building, but how we were structuring it, how we were building it.
paragraphs.cn: 这从一开始就是我们商业计划的一部分。我们与 Deck Nine 外部合作开发《苍穹浩瀚》，因为我们意识到，试图从零开始同时制作两款游戏并不是一个好计划。我们一直明白必须专注于自己能做的事情。我们花费的时间比预期的要长，因为我们在2022年进行了一次技术重置。我们最初使用的是遗留的 Telltale 工具和 Unreal Engine 4，从纸面上看，这似乎能带来各种良好的生产收益、工具、可扩展性和迭代能力。但几年后，现实变得显而易见：「不，这行不通。」于是我们重置了管线，在如何构建内容方面回到了起点——不是构建什么内容，而是如何组织、如何构建。

paragraphs.en: There's a lot of cinematic minutes that go into a Telltale game and building those at quality [and] efficiently is kind of the central problem to how you run the company.
paragraphs.cn: 一款 Telltale 游戏包含大量的过场动画分钟数，而高质量、高效率地制作这些内容，正是公司运营的核心问题。

paragraphs.en: So, Telltale still has its own development staff?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，Telltale 仍然拥有自己的开发人员吗？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: [It's] 40% internal, 60% external from a rule of thumb standpoint. We work with Trick. We also have a couple subject matter experts for some things like character rigging — it's super important and super specialized, but you also only need it for a [specific] period. And when you're done with it, those are very expensive, very talented people that don't have a lot to do for the rest of the title. So stuff like that is outsourced in the traditional way [to] subject matter experts and then Trick and Telltale have structured an integrated team. We don't do traditional co-dev that's asset based or output based. We run one team, and where those people happen to collect their paychecks is irrelevant from a team structure standpoint. It all works under the same management system, the same standups, the same milestone basis, the same sprints. And so that's been a really effective way for us to work together. We've invested a couple years in our relationship with Trick, getting to the point where we can function this way.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：粗略估计，内部占40%，外部占60%。我们与 Trick 合作。我们还有一些特定领域的专家，比如角色绑定——这非常重要且非常专业，但你也只在特定时期需要。一旦完成，这些非常昂贵、非常有才华的人在项目的其余部分就没有太多事情可做了。所以这类工作会以传统方式外包给领域专家，然后 Trick 和 Telltale 组建了一个整合团队。我们不采用传统的基于资产或产出的联合开发模式。我们运行一个团队，从团队结构的角度来看，这些人在哪里领薪水并不重要。所有工作都在同一个管理系统、同一个每日站会、同一个里程碑基础、同一个冲刺周期下进行。这对我们来说是一种非常有效的合作方式。我们花了几年时间与 Trick 建立关系，才达到能够以这种方式运作的程度。

paragraphs.en: How did the Wolf Among Us Remastered project get started?
paragraphs.cn: 《我们身边的狼》重制版项目是如何启动的？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: Wolf 1 has been, because it's on the legacy engine and a bespoke code base, it is harder to work with than doing stuff from scratch, just because there's a great deal of institutional knowledge that's necessary to be able to wield those tools. We're about a year and a half into the journey on the remaster for Wolf 1. It really started out of 'we need to update this to keep it current on consoles and functioning and there are some bugs here that are pretty egregious that would be nice to get rid of as we're playing through this.' And then we did some experiments with, is it possible to make it look incrementally better and what would that look like? And so Wolf 1 just kind of grew organically out of that, wanting to continue to support it, wanting to see it stay in the market and be a really clean experience for new players that come in and want to play through it.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：《狼1》因为使用的是遗留引擎和定制代码库，所以比从头开始做还要困难，因为需要大量的机构知识才能熟练运用这些工具。我们进行《狼1》重制版的工作已经大约一年半了。它实际上始于「我们需要更新它，使其能在现代主机上运行并正常工作，而且游戏中存在一些相当严重的 bug，在游玩过程中如果能消除它们就好了。」然后我们做了一些实验，看看是否有可能让它看起来更好，以及会是什么样子。于是《狼1》重制版就这样自然而然地发展起来了——我们希望继续支持它，希望它能在市场上留存，并为想要游玩的新玩家提供非常干净的体验。

paragraphs.en: And then, you called it out earlier, we're doing the sequel. The best way to introduce people to the sequel is to let them play the first title. And so it made business sense, it made production sense. It made sense as fans of the content and materials to do it. So that's been effectively moving forward, a very slow burn with the right approach, training new folks to work with the tools and tech and standing that stuff back up and ensuring that we could do it effectively.
paragraphs.cn: 然后，正如你之前提到的，我们正在制作续作。向人们介绍续作的最佳方式就是让他们先玩初代。所以这在商业上、制作上都是合理的。作为内容和素材的粉丝，这样做也是合理的。因此，我们一直在稳步推进，以正确的方式慢慢来，培训新人使用工具和技术，重新搭建这些东西，并确保我们能够有效地完成。

paragraphs.en: How was development impacted by the Ad Hoc leaving? Can you talk about why that partnership ended, and how far it got?
paragraphs.cn: Ad Hoc 的离开对开发有何影响？你能谈谈合作终止的原因，以及合作进展到了什么程度吗？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: We were working with Ad Hoc in the same way we were working with Trick, as an integrated team. It was not like a Deck Nine situation where we had an [external] developer. When we started working with Ad Hoc, they were relatively new. They were effectively four people, three of which had worked on Wolf 1. So it was natural for us to work together. When we did the tech reset and we realized that we're not going to be producing content for at least a year in terms of how we're approaching this, it didn't make sense for us to keep them tied up and effectively sitting on the sidelines not doing a lot while we went through the pipeline and [worked out] how we were going to build the content. And so it sort of came to a natural break point based upon the reality of where we were from a pipeline standpoint and they obviously had desires to ship their own games and did not want to be idle either.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：我们与 Ad Hoc 的合作方式与 Trick 相同，都是作为一个整合团队。这与 Deck Nine 的情况不同，后者是外部开发商。我们开始与 Ad Hoc 合作时，他们相对较新。实际上只有四个人，其中三人曾参与过《狼1》的开发。所以我们合作是很自然的。当我们进行技术重置，意识到至少在一年内我们无法以这种方式制作内容时，继续让他们被束缚住、实际上在一边无所事事，而我们则忙于管线建设和确定如何构建内容，这就不合理了。因此，基于管线方面的现实情况，我们自然走到了一个分岔点，而他们显然也有推出自己游戏的愿望，不想闲着。

paragraphs.en: And I don't know if you know the core history of the team, but the core group's been together for 11 years across multiple companies on a journey to ship games and they deserved to be able to go out and do that and make that happen. So we just sort of got to a place where it didn't make sense for us to keep them tied up, as much as we wanted to collaborate on this title. And then by the time we were ready, they were off doing Dispatch as we all know now and it wasn't really effective to reintegrate them into the team. Unfortunately, timing works that way sometimes.
paragraphs.cn: 我不知道你是否了解这个团队的核心历史，但核心团队已经在一起11年了，跨越多家公司，致力于推出游戏，他们理应能够走出去实现这一目标。所以我们只是到了一个不再适合继续束缚他们的节点，尽管我们很想在这款游戏上继续合作。而当我们准备好时，他们已经在做《超英派遣中心》了，正如我们现在所知，重新将他们整合到团队中并不现实。不幸的是，时机有时就是这样。

paragraphs.en: It sounds like from your side, the reset was just a technical thing you wanted to do.
paragraphs.cn: 听起来从你们的角度来看，重置只是一个你们想做的技术性调整。

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: It was the reality that we weren't going to ship on the path we were on, we were not going to ship a game worthy of the name [The Wolf Among Us] based upon how the content was coming together, what the pipeline looked like. It was a hard choice to do a reset. Nobody wants to do that. I'm not saying it was easy on anybody, I guess is what I'm trying to say. It's not the decision anybody wanted to make, but I think we all understood the nature of the decision of why it had to be made.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：现实是，按照我们当时的路径，我们无法推出游戏，无法推出一款配得上《我们身边的狼》这个名字的游戏——基于内容的整合方式和管线的状况。重置是一个艰难的选择。没有人愿意这么做。我想说的是，这对任何人来说都不容易。这不是任何人想要做出的决定，但我认为我们都理解这个决定的性质，以及为什么必须做出这个决定。

paragraphs.en: Can you shed any light on how much of AdHoc's work remains in the game?
paragraphs.cn: 你能透露一下 AdHoc 的工作有多少保留在游戏中吗？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: Certainly there's some pieces of it that are there, but at the end of the day, when you do a complete reset, you have to reset with the creative leadership you have and how you're building the game. So it certainly isn't the same game as envisioned that was there. Some of the gains we were expecting on the tools and pipeline were about how much content we could create in a given period of time on a given budget number and that wasn't going to come to fruition either. So the number of minutes and the scoping had to be different than where we were in terms of how we approached the content. There are elements of it, but it's a different game is probably the best answer that I can give. There are people that have been working on the title from its inception consistently. So there is some continuity in the team, and it's very difficult to draw like a clean line, 'Oh, this is the Ad Hoc version. This is what was going on here.' It was always a collaborative approach to how the content was being built.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：当然有一些部分保留了下来，但归根结底，当你进行完全重置时，你必须根据现有的创意领导团队以及你构建游戏的方式来重置。所以这肯定不是当初设想的那款游戏了。我们原本期望在工具和管线方面获得的一些收益，涉及在给定预算下、给定时间内能制作多少内容，但这些也没有实现。因此，过场动画的分钟数和范围必须与我们之前处理内容的方式有所不同。其中有它的元素，但这是一款不同的游戏——这大概是我能给出的最好答案。有一些人从项目一开始就一直在参与。所以团队有一定的连续性，很难划出一条清晰的界线说「哦，这是 Ad Hoc 的版本，这是当时的情况。」内容的构建始终是一种协作方式。

paragraphs.en: Is there anything more you want to say now, to reassure fans after this bumpy development process? Obviously Dispatch is now out, and has been this huge success which is great to see.
paragraphs.cn: 在经历了这段坎坷的开发历程之后，你现在还有什么想对粉丝们说的，以让他们放心吗？显然《超英派遣中心》已经发售，并且取得了巨大成功，这令人欣慰。

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: It's a great game. I would say internally we're thrilled that Ad Hoc got to go away and make Dispatch. The weird thing about narrative [games] is that none of us really think of each other as competitors. We're in such a niche piece of content that there's definitely a sprite of core in terms of people who make this kind of content. We're all rooting for each other. In terms of reassuring the fans, let's show you what we have at Summer Games Fest. We're pretty confident what we have will make Wolf two fans happy. We've shown it to enough people to know that we're on the right path here. We get a very positive response from when we take people into what we're doing right now.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：这是一款很棒的游戏。我想说，在内部，我们为 Ad Hoc 能够离开并制作出《超英派遣中心》感到兴奋。叙事游戏的一个奇怪之处在于，我们并不真正把彼此视为竞争对手。我们处于一个非常小众的内容领域，制作这类内容的人之间确实有一种核心精神。我们都在为彼此加油。至于让粉丝放心，我们会在夏日游戏节上展示我们的成果。我们非常有信心，我们所展示的内容会让《狼2》的粉丝们满意。我们已经向足够多的人展示过，知道我们走在正确的道路上。当我们向人们展示我们目前正在做的事情时，得到了非常积极的反馈。

paragraphs.en: We are trying to be good stewards of the IP and the universe and everything that's there. While from the outside it seems like we've been quiet and people have had their doubts about it, for the last seven years there isn't a person on my team that hasn't made this the priority professionally in their lives to get this game done. The amount of sacrifice and creativity that has been necessary to get us this far is incredible in terms of the team that's there. There's a great deal of passion, care, and love for this franchise in this team that is still there building it.
paragraphs.cn: 我们努力成为这个 IP、这个宇宙及其一切的好管家。虽然从外部看来，我们一直保持沉默，人们对此有所怀疑，但在过去的七年里，我团队中的每一个人都将完成这款游戏作为他们职业生涯中的首要任务。为了走到今天，团队所付出的牺牲和创造力是令人难以置信的。这个仍在建设中的团队，对这个系列充满了热情、关怀和爱。

paragraphs.en: If everything goes well, what is your ambition for Telltale next? Is it more remasters, more sequels? Is there a particular focus? Is it Wolf 3? Is it bringing back another popular franchise from Telltale's past? Where would you love to go?
paragraphs.cn: 如果一切顺利，你对 Telltale 下一步的雄心是什么？是更多重制版、更多续作？有特定的重点吗？是《狼3》？还是带回 Telltale 过去另一个受欢迎的系列？你希望走向何方？

paragraphs.en: Ottilie: We are actively looking at what we'll do next. We're working on what we're calling a sequential slate, right? Instead of going wide, we're doing things in parallel, one thing in a time. We'll choose what we're doing next this year and have it in concept development while we're finishing up Wolf 2 so we know where we're moving to when we're done. There are quite a few games in contention for what that might be. It won't be Wolf 3 out of the gate. I mean, there needs to be some breathing room between sequels. Certainly we'd love to do Wolf 3, but I think that's a 'ship this one, probably ship something else in between, come back to Wolf', that kind of cadence. In a perfect world, we'd ship a new game every two years or so, taking about three years to make it, starting about a year prior so that there's a little bit of overlap with concept teams.
paragraphs.cn: Ottilie：我们正在积极考虑下一步做什么。我们正在制定所谓的「顺序计划」，对吧？不是全面铺开，而是并行推进，一次只做一件事。我们将在今年内选择下一步做什么，并在完成《狼2》的同时进行概念开发，这样我们就能知道完成后该往哪个方向走。有好几款游戏在候选之列。不会一上来就是《狼3》。我的意思是，续作之间需要一些喘息空间。我们当然很想做《狼3》，但我认为节奏应该是「先推出这一部，中间可能推出其他作品，然后再回到《狼》系列」。在理想情况下，我们大约每两年推出一款新游戏，制作周期大约三年，提前一年开始，以便与概念团队有一些重叠。

manual_approved_issues.detail: opus_summary must be 70-80 non-space characters, got 92
